ZEAL Ear-Number (EN) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER

  • Show All Code
  • Hide All Code
  • View Source

The ear-number analog of the PH/EH notebooks: same SNP50K ancestry pipeline, phenotype = per-field SpATS ear-number BLUEs, candidate set = the curated maize ear-number / prolificacy cloned-gene panel (core gt1/tb1/tru1 + inflorescence-architecture branching/meristem/KRN genes). Significance is a genome-wide FWER from a 1000-permutation null (per scan): the Manhattan line is the −log₁₀P exceeded by only 5% of permutations, and candidate/peak calls report whether they clear it.

Author

Fausto Rodríguez-Zapata

Published

July 16, 2026

What this is. The ear-number (EN) sibling of zeal-qtl-recovery-eh-mlm-snp50k, built from the same real ZEAL pipeline end-to-end: field EN → SpATS spatial BLUEs → SNP50K teosinte-ancestry dosage → RTIGER ancestry mosaic → K → JLM + MLM. The population is the same near-isogenic B73×teosinte BC2S3 lines from 5 teosinte taxa. EN is ear number — the count of ears borne per plant — and the per-genotype value is the per-field SpATS mean across the two fields (CLY23-D4, CLY25-B5). Only the phenotype and the candidate panel change vs the EH notebook. A closely related prolificacy (Prolif) notebook shares this candidate panel.

ImportantGenotype at 0.4× skim: why ancestry, not per-SNP

SNP50K is 51,991 teosinte-vs-B73 sites pileup-genotyped at ~0.4× — hard genotypes are unusable (70% missing, ~0% hom-ALT), so ancestry is inferred from the per-sample allele counts. The per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior) (Panel B) is therefore λ_GC-deflated (sparse mean-imputation); the RTIGER ancestry mosaic (Panel C) is the usable, block-smoothed genotype (teosinte presence 0.111, matching the established BzeaSeq ~0.101). This is the SNP50K-specific reason ancestry inference is mandatory, and is identical to the DTA/PH/EH notebooks — only the phenotype changes.

Trait context. Ear number / prolificacy is a domestication-and-inflorescence-architecture trait: teosinte is highly branched and prolific (many small ears on long lateral branches), while B73 has strong apical dominance and typically one or two ears. The trait is therefore expected to respond strongly to teosinte introgression. The candidate panel is the curated maize ear-number / prolificacy cloned-gene set, split into two tiers: the core ear-number regulators — gt1 (grassy tillers1; HD-ZIP I TF underlying the prol1.1 QTL, represses axillary ear-bud growth, teosinte allele → prolific ears), tb1 (teosinte branched1; the classic domestication TCP TF whose Hopscotch insertion drives apical dominance and reduced ear number), and tru1 (tassels replace upper ears1; a direct tb1 target promoting ear-vs-tassel identity) — and a second tier of inflorescence-architecture / meristem / kernel-row-number genes (ub2/ub3 SBP-box branching, ra1/ra2/ra3 ramosa branch determinacy, fea2/fea3/td1 CLV–WUS meristem-size receptors). Because teosinte ancestry is broadly ear-number-increasing and partly collinear with K, the mixed model absorbs ancestry-correlated variance, and the model-independent JLM (nested-in-family stepwise) is the primary QTL tool — with gt1/tb1 the two loci most likely to surface as headline, effect-bearing QTL rather than polygenic background.

Show code 
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R")) # shared: plot_manhattan/lollipop, lambda_gc, LOD
Show code 
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/en_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel C
gt <- fread(here("data/zeal/en_gwas_mlm_hwe_post_gt_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel B
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/en_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/en_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}
# Significance = genome-wide FWER from a 1000-permutation null (scripts/zeal_gwas_perm.R),
# stored per (trait, model, geno) in data/zeal/gwas_perm_thresholds.csv.
TR <- "EN"
thr <- function(model, geno, a = 0.05) gwas_thr(TR, model, geno, a)
thr_ols <- thr("ols", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_mlm <- thr("mlm", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_gt <- thr("mlm", "hwe_post_gt")
nsig <- function(p, t) sum(-log10(p) > t, na.rm = TRUE)
NoteSignificance = 1000-permutation genome-wide FWER (5%)

Every Manhattan’s dotted line is the −log₁₀P exceeded by only 5% of 1000 permutations of that scan (a genome-wide max-−log₁₀P null; scripts/zeal_gwas_perm.R), stored per (trait, model, geno) in data/zeal/gwas_perm_thresholds.csv. A marker is significant iff its −log₁₀P clears that line. The line’s height differs per panel with the null. Candidate/peak tables report whether the marker clears the 5% FWER line.

Candidate gene registry

Curated evidence for each candidate (tier, causal direction, evidence type, phenotype, and a DOI link) from the tracked registry (reference/candidate_genes.csv × candidate_evidence.csv). These are the genes starred on the Manhattans and labelled on the lollipop; loci without B73 v5 coordinates still appear here but are not plotted.

Show code 
candidate_table(TR)
Candidate genes for EN from the tracked registry (reference/candidate_*.csv): 12 loci (12 mapped to B73 v5, 0 unmapped). DOI links where curated; “TODO” = evidence not yet curated.
gene full name locus tier direction evidence phenotype / note reference
gt1 grassy tillers1 1:23.4 Mb 1 - ear number / prolificacy (core) Ear number / prolificacy — underlies the prol1.1 QTL; represses growth of axillary ear buds. Repressed by shade; teosinte allele → prolific tillers/ears. 10.1073/pnas.1102819108
ub2 unbranched2 1:192.4 Mb 2 - inflorescence architecture (branching / meristem / KRN) Inflorescence branching / kernel row number — tassel branch & ear row number. 10.1073/pnas.1407401112
tb1 teosinte branched1 1:272.3 Mb 1 - ear number / prolificacy (core) Ear number / prolificacy — suppresses axillary branching; Hopscotch insertion upstream drives apical dominance & reduced ear number. Major domestication locus. 10.1038/386485a0
abi17 ABI3-VP1-transcription factor 17 2:49.4 Mb 3 - chr2 positional candidate Ear number / prolificacy — chr2 positional candidate (abi17, MaizeGDB). Browser/MaizeMine ‘Auxin response factor’ auto-annotation is a B3-domain artifact. MaizeGDB (Zm00001eb082150 = abi17); positional candidate under chr2 QTL
ra2 ramosa2 3:12.8 Mb 2 - inflorescence architecture (branching / meristem / KRN) Inflorescence branching — meristem determinacy in ear/tassel. 10.1105/tpc.105.039032
fea3 fasciated ear3 3:29.2 Mb 2 - inflorescence architecture (branching / meristem / KRN) Ear meristem size / kernel row number — CLE-peptide signalling. 10.1038/ng.3567
tru1 tassels replace upper ears1 3:152.1 Mb 1 - ear number / prolificacy (core) Ear number / prolificacy — direct target of tb1; promotes ear (vs tassel) identity in axillary meristems. Loss → tassels replace upper ears. 10.1073/pnas.1714960114
fea2 fasciated ear2 4:138.7 Mb 2 - inflorescence architecture (branching / meristem / KRN) Ear meristem size / kernel row & ear fasciation — CLV-WUS pathway. 10.1101/gad.208501
ub3 unbranched3 4:205.4 Mb 2 - inflorescence architecture (branching / meristem / KRN) Inflorescence branching / kernel row number — paralog of ub2. 10.1073/pnas.1407401112
td1 thick tassel dwarf1 5:63.5 Mb 2 - inflorescence architecture (branching / meristem / KRN) Ear/tassel meristem size — CLV-WUS pathway; kernel row number. 10.1242/dev.01671
ra1 ramosa1 7:115.0 Mb 2 - inflorescence architecture (branching / meristem / KRN) Inflorescence branching — restricts long-branch (spikelet-pair) formation; affects ear/tassel branch number. 10.1038/nature03892
ra3 ramosa3 7:176.0 Mb 2 - inflorescence architecture (branching / meristem / KRN) Inflorescence branching — meristem determinacy; sugar signalling. 10.1038/nature04725

Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker

For each maize ear-number / prolificacy candidate (B73 v5), the closest marker to the gene body vs the highest −log₁₀P marker within ±500 kb in the RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Family+K) scan (Panel C). Candidates are the curated cloned ear-number / prolificacy panel (core gt1/tb1/tru1 + inflorescence-architecture branching/meristem/KRN genes; v5 coordinates); pathway records the tier and protein/role each acts in.

Show code 
sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/en_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)][, qtl := ifelse(qtl_chen2019 == "" | is.na(qtl_chen2019), paste0("EN(chr", chr, ")"), qtl_chen2019)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], pathway = gg$pathway[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `clears perm 5%` = if (nrow(w)) (w$logP[kw] >= thr_mlm) else NA
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),
  caption = sprintf("EN candidates (B73 v5; curated maize ear-number / prolificacy panel): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Family+K) scan; 'clears perm 5%%' = window-max exceeds the 1000-permutation FWER line (-log10P = %.2f).", thr_mlm)
)
EN candidates (B73 v5; curated maize ear-number / prolificacy panel): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Family+K) scan; ‘clears perm 5%’ = window-max exceeds the 1000-permutation FWER line (-log10P = 4.75).
gene pathway chr gene start (v5) closest Δstart (bp) closest -log10P window-max -log10P clears perm 5%
gt1 Ear number / prolificacy — underlies the prol1.1 QTL; represses growth of axillary ear buds. Repressed by shade; teosinte allele → prolific tillers/ears. 1 23,433,554 -1,058 3.61 5.23 TRUE
ub2 Inflorescence branching / kernel row number — tassel branch & ear row number. 1 192,381,440 -101,208 0.24 0.82 FALSE
tb1 Ear number / prolificacy — suppresses axillary branching; Hopscotch insertion upstream drives apical dominance & reduced ear number. Major domestication locus. 1 272,330,564 -40,634 0.06 0.15 FALSE
abi17 Ear number / prolificacy — chr2 positional candidate (abi17, MaizeGDB). Browser/MaizeMine ‘Auxin response factor’ auto-annotation is a B3-domain artifact. 2 49,388,328 2,821 0.32 0.54 FALSE
ra2 Inflorescence branching — meristem determinacy in ear/tassel. 3 12,830,057 7,984 1.55 1.64 FALSE
fea3 Ear meristem size / kernel row number — CLE-peptide signalling. 3 29,213,107 -40,521 0.18 0.48 FALSE
tru1 Ear number / prolificacy — direct target of tb1; promotes ear (vs tassel) identity in axillary meristems. Loss → tassels replace upper ears. 3 152,086,191 -7,157 0.03 0.45 FALSE
fea2 Ear meristem size / kernel row & ear fasciation — CLV-WUS pathway. 4 138,680,814 -5,446 0.03 0.07 FALSE
ub3 Inflorescence branching / kernel row number — paralog of ub2. 4 205,414,194 13,112 1.67 1.70 FALSE
td1 Ear/tassel meristem size — CLV-WUS pathway; kernel row number. 5 63,488,542 -1,077 0.06 0.16 FALSE
ra1 Inflorescence branching — restricts long-branch (spikelet-pair) formation; affects ear/tassel branch number. 7 114,958,643 29,660 0.35 0.58 FALSE
ra3 Inflorescence branching — meristem determinacy; sugar signalling. 7 175,982,697 3,277 0.68 0.92 FALSE

OLS → MLM: structure control

Here Family is the 82 donor-accession fixed factor (parent-plant-level structure correction) that the MLM and per-marker OLS scans were actually computed with — a stronger correction than the 5 donor taxa the JLM nests on. It likely over-corrects population structure and costs some power; a taxon-level (5) MLM/OLS rerun is a pending comparison (see TERMINOLOGY.md).

The fixed-effect OLS (Family + marker, no K) vs the MLM (Family + K) on the RTIGER mosaic, same fixed part. OLS is intrinsically inflated (ancestry-block LD, unfixable by any fixed factor); K deflates λ toward 1 and removes the ancestry-correlated bulk that a near-isogenic, teosinte-ancestry-structured ear-number panel carries.

Show code 
plot_manhattan(ols,
  sprintf("ZEAL EN — OLS (Family + marker, no K), RTIGER mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = perm FWER 5%%)", lam(ols$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_ols,
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/en_gwas_ols_manhattan_fdr_snp50k.png")
)

The OLS Manhattan (no K) is genome-wide inflated (ancestry-block LD) — the same structure artifact the MLM K term removes below.

Show code 
lam_tab <- data.table(
  model = c("OLS (Family + marker, no K)", "MLM (Family + K)"),
  markers = c(nrow(ols), nrow(mlm)),
  lambda_GC = c(lam(ols$P), lam(mlm$P)),
  `n > perm 5%` = c(nsig(ols$P, thr_ols), nsig(mlm$P, thr_mlm)),
  max_logP = round(c(max(-log10(ols$P)), max(-log10(mlm$P))), 2)
)
knitr::kable(lam_tab, caption = "OLS vs MLM on the RTIGER mosaic (same Family fixed part): K deflates genomic inflation; n markers above each scan's 1000-permutation 5% FWER line.")
OLS vs MLM on the RTIGER mosaic (same Family fixed part): K deflates genomic inflation; n markers above each scan’s 1000-permutation 5% FWER line.
model markers lambda_GC n > perm 5% max_logP
OLS (Family + marker, no K) 48900 9.19 11491 15.32
MLM (Family + K) 48900 1.04 1 5.23
Show code 
ov <- fread(overlap)
peak_at <- function(scan, ch, s) {
  w <- scan[CHR == ch & abs(BP - s) <= 5e5 & is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  if (!nrow(w)) NA_real_ else round(max(-log10(w$P)), 2)
}
cmp <- ov[, .(
  gene = symbol, chr,
  `OLS peak` = mapply(peak_at, list(ols), chr, start),
  `MLM peak` = mapply(peak_at, list(mlm), chr, start)
)]
cmp[, `drop (OLS−MLM)` := round(`OLS peak` - `MLM peak`, 2)]
setorder(cmp, -`OLS peak`)
knitr::kable(cmp, caption = "Local peak -log10P (±500 kb) per candidate: OLS vs MLM. K suppresses the ancestry-collinear ear-number signal.")
Local peak -log10P (±500 kb) per candidate: OLS vs MLM. K suppresses the ancestry-collinear ear-number signal.
gene chr OLS peak MLM peak drop (OLS−MLM)
gt1 1 15.32 5.23 10.09
ra2 3 6.91 1.64 5.27
ub3 4 6.68 1.70 4.98
fea2 4 5.13 0.07 5.06
ra3 7 4.96 0.92 4.04
abi17 2 3.66 0.54 3.12
fea3 3 2.76 0.48 2.28
ub2 1 2.35 0.82 1.53
td1 5 2.29 0.16 2.13
tru1 3 1.95 0.45 1.50
ra1 7 0.62 0.58 0.04
tb1 1 0.40 0.15 0.25

JLM QTL genomic distribution (Panel A)

EN QTL from JLM (TASSEL StepwiseOLS, nested in taxon, entry by a 1000-permutation empirical α = 0.05 (Chen-style genome-wide FWER; per-trait)). JLM QTL are selected by the stepwise enter/exit P (a model-selection gate), independent of the per-scan permutation FWER line used for the GWAS Manhattans below. Model-independent — the primary EN analysis, as in Chen 2019.

Show code 
p_lolli <- plot_lollipop(
  here("results/sim/zeal/tassel/en_jlm_native1.txt"), mlm,
  "ZEAL EN — JLM QTL genomic distribution (nested in taxon)",
  overlap_csv = overlap,
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/en_jlm_lollipop_snp50k.png")
)
p_lolli

MLM (Family + K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (Panel C)

Show code 
p_mlm <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("ZEAL EN — MLM (Family + K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = perm FWER 5%%)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_mlm, out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/en_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_manhattan_fdr_snp50k.png")
)
p_mlm
Show code 
cat(sprintf("%d markers clear the 5%% FWER line (-log10P = %.2f)\n", nsig(mlm$P, thr_mlm), thr_mlm))
1 markers clear the 5% FWER line (-log10P = 4.75)

Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)

Panel B (per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior)) is λ_GC-deflated — the 70%-missing per-SNP panel at 0.4× is sparse — but catches sharp SNP hits the mosaic block-terraces away. Panel C (the ancestry mosaic) is the usable, well-calibrated genotype. The B↔︎C contrast is the notebook’s per-SNP-scatter vs ancestry-block-smoothing, and the demonstration that ancestry inference is mandatory at low coverage — identical in mechanism to the DTA/PH/EH notebooks.

Show code 
p_B <- plot_manhattan(gt,
  sprintf("EN — MLM (Family+K), per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior) (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(gt$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_gt
)
p_C <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("EN — MLM (Family+K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_mlm
)
composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(p_lolli, p_B, p_C, ncol = 1, labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 20, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/en_composite_snp50k.png"), composite, width = 9, height = 13, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
composite

Multi-caller EN scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)

The same EN MLM (Family+K) scan on each ancestry caller’s recovered genotypes, at the real ~0.4× depth (not a coverage sweep). The robust, trait-independent finding is teosinte-presence recovery: the block/bin-aggregated callers (RTIGER 0.111; binhmm 0.101) recover the established BzeaSeq fraction (~0.10), while per-SNP calling (control) and the per-SNP HMM recalls (nNIL, LB-Impute) under-call. Genomic inflation is phenotype-dependent (reported per caller below). Caller genotypes are population-level and trait-independent; only the phenotype (EN) changes.

Show code 
callers <- c(control = "hwe_post_gt", nNIL = "nnil_mosaic", RTIGER = "rtiger_mosaic", `LB-Impute` = "lbimpute_mosaic", binhmm = "binhmm_mosaic")
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/en_candidate_genes.tsv"))[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
# best (max -log10P) EN candidate window per caller
max_cand_logp <- function(sc) {
  lp <- -log10(sc$P)
  vals <- unlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
    idx <- which(sc$CHR == gg$chr[i] & abs(sc$BP - gg$start[i]) <= 5e5)
    if (length(idx)) max(lp[idx], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_
  }))
  if (all(is.na(vals))) NA_real_ else round(max(vals, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
}
ctab2 <- rbindlist(lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  g <- callers[[nm]]
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/en_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", g)))
  mf <- here(sprintf("data/zeal/zeal_%s.rds", g))
  pres <- if (file.exists(mf)) {
    st <- readRDS(mf)$state
    round(mean(st %in% c(1L, 2L), na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }
  sc <- sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  data.table(
    caller = nm, `teosinte presence` = pres, lambda_GC = lam(sc$P),
    `n > perm 5%` = nsig(sc$P, gwas_thr(TR, "mlm", g)),
    `best candidate -log10P` = max_cand_logp(sc)
  )
}))
knitr::kable(ctab2, caption = "EN MLM (Family+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers above the caller's own 1000-permutation 5% FWER line and the best (max) -log10P across the EN ear-number / prolificacy candidate windows.")
EN MLM (Family+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers above the caller’s own 1000-permutation 5% FWER line and the best (max) -log10P across the EN ear-number / prolificacy candidate windows.
caller teosinte presence lambda_GC n > perm 5% best candidate -log10P
control 0.008 0.87 3 3.64
nNIL 0.018 0.93 2 6.93
RTIGER 0.111 1.04 1 5.23
LB-Impute 0.035 0.97 169 1.74
binhmm 0.101 1.23 65 2.30
Show code 
panels <- lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/en_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", callers[[nm]])))
  plot_manhattan(sc, sprintf("EN MLM (Family+K) — %s (λ_GC = %.2f; perm FWER 5%%)", nm, lam(sc$P)), overlap_csv = overlap, lod = gwas_thr(TR, "mlm", callers[[nm]]))
})
cc <- cowplot::plot_grid(plotlist = panels, ncol = 1, labels = LETTERS[1:5], label_size = 18, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/en_caller_composite_fdr_snp50k.png"), cc, width = 9, height = 20, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
cc
Source Code
---
title: "ZEAL Ear-Number (EN) GWAS + JLM on the SNP50K panel — 1000-permutation FWER"
subtitle: "The ear-number analog of the PH/EH notebooks: same SNP50K ancestry pipeline, phenotype = per-field SpATS ear-number BLUEs, candidate set = the curated maize ear-number / prolificacy cloned-gene panel (core gt1/tb1/tru1 + inflorescence-architecture branching/meristem/KRN genes). Significance is a genome-wide FWER from a 1000-permutation null (per scan): the Manhattan line is the −log₁₀P exceeded by only 5% of permutations, and candidate/peak calls report whether they clear it."
author: "Fausto Rodríguez-Zapata"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
  echo: true
---

**What this is.** The ear-number (EN) sibling of
[`zeal-qtl-recovery-eh-mlm-snp50k`](zeal-qtl-recovery-eh-mlm-snp50k.html), built from the
same real ZEAL pipeline end-to-end: field EN → SpATS spatial BLUEs → SNP50K teosinte-ancestry
dosage → RTIGER ancestry mosaic → K → JLM + MLM. The population is the same near-isogenic
B73×teosinte BC2S3 lines from **5 teosinte taxa**. EN is **ear number** — the count of ears borne
per plant — and the per-genotype value is the per-field SpATS mean across the two fields (CLY23-D4,
CLY25-B5). Only the phenotype and the candidate panel change vs the EH notebook. A closely related
[`prolificacy (Prolif)`](zeal-qtl-recovery-prolif-mlm-snp50k.html) notebook shares this candidate
panel.

::: {.callout-important}
## Genotype at 0.4× skim: why ancestry, not per-SNP
SNP50K is 51,991 teosinte-vs-B73 sites pileup-genotyped at ~0.4× — hard genotypes are
unusable (70% missing, ~0% hom-ALT), so ancestry is inferred from the per-sample allele
**counts**. The **per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior) (Panel B)** is therefore λ_GC-deflated (sparse
mean-imputation); the **RTIGER ancestry mosaic (Panel C)** is the usable, block-smoothed
genotype (teosinte presence 0.111, matching the established BzeaSeq ~0.101). This is the
SNP50K-specific reason ancestry inference is mandatory, and is identical to the DTA/PH/EH notebooks —
only the phenotype changes.
:::

**Trait context.** Ear number / prolificacy is a **domestication-and-inflorescence-architecture**
trait: teosinte is highly branched and prolific (many small ears on long lateral branches), while
B73 has strong apical dominance and typically one or two ears. The trait is therefore expected to
respond strongly to teosinte introgression. The candidate panel is the curated maize ear-number /
prolificacy cloned-gene set, split into two tiers: the **core ear-number regulators** — `gt1`
(*grassy tillers1*; HD-ZIP I TF underlying the **prol1.1** QTL, represses axillary ear-bud growth,
teosinte allele → prolific ears), `tb1` (*teosinte branched1*; the classic domestication TCP TF whose
*Hopscotch* insertion drives apical dominance and reduced ear number), and `tru1` (*tassels replace
upper ears1*; a direct tb1 target promoting ear-vs-tassel identity) — and a second tier of
**inflorescence-architecture / meristem / kernel-row-number** genes (`ub2`/`ub3` SBP-box branching,
`ra1`/`ra2`/`ra3` ramosa branch determinacy, `fea2`/`fea3`/`td1` CLV–WUS meristem-size receptors).
Because teosinte ancestry is broadly ear-number-increasing and **partly collinear with K**, the
mixed model absorbs ancestry-correlated variance, and the model-independent **JLM** (nested-in-family
stepwise) is the primary QTL tool — with `gt1`/`tb1` the two loci most likely to surface as
headline, effect-bearing QTL rather than polygenic background.

```{r setup}
library(here)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(fastman)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggtext)
library(scales)
library(cowplot)
source(here("scripts/teonam_notebook_plots.R")) # shared: plot_manhattan/lollipop, lambda_gc, LOD
```

```{r load}
mlm <- fread(here("data/zeal/en_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel C
gt <- fread(here("data/zeal/en_gwas_mlm_hwe_post_gt_donor_accession_snp50k.csv")) # Panel B
ols <- fread(here("data/zeal/en_gwas_ols_donor_accession_snp50k.csv"))[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
overlap <- here("results/sim/zeal/en_candidate_overlap.csv")
lam <- function(p) {
  p <- p[is.finite(p) & p > 0 & p <= 1]
  round(median(qchisq(p, 1, lower.tail = FALSE)) / qchisq(0.5, 1), 2)
}
# Significance = genome-wide FWER from a 1000-permutation null (scripts/zeal_gwas_perm.R),
# stored per (trait, model, geno) in data/zeal/gwas_perm_thresholds.csv.
TR <- "EN"
thr <- function(model, geno, a = 0.05) gwas_thr(TR, model, geno, a)
thr_ols <- thr("ols", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_mlm <- thr("mlm", "rtiger_mosaic")
thr_gt <- thr("mlm", "hwe_post_gt")
nsig <- function(p, t) sum(-log10(p) > t, na.rm = TRUE)
```

::: {.callout-note}
## Significance = 1000-permutation genome-wide FWER (5%)
Every Manhattan's dotted line is the **−log₁₀P exceeded by only 5% of 1000 permutations** of
that scan (a genome-wide max-−log₁₀P null; `scripts/zeal_gwas_perm.R`), stored per
(trait, model, geno) in `data/zeal/gwas_perm_thresholds.csv`. A marker is significant iff its
−log₁₀P clears that line. The line's height differs per panel with the null. Candidate/peak
tables report whether the marker clears the 5% FWER line.
:::

## Candidate gene registry

Curated evidence for each **candidate** (tier, causal direction, evidence type, phenotype, and a DOI link) from the tracked registry (`reference/candidate_genes.csv` × `candidate_evidence.csv`). These are the genes starred on the Manhattans and labelled on the lollipop; loci without B73 v5 coordinates still appear here but are not plotted.

```{r candidate-registry}
candidate_table(TR)
```

## Candidate genes: closest marker vs window-max marker

For each maize ear-number / prolificacy candidate (B73 v5), the closest marker to the gene body vs
the highest −log₁₀P marker within ±500 kb in the RTIGER-mosaic **MLM (Family+K)** scan (Panel C).
Candidates are the curated cloned ear-number / prolificacy panel (core `gt1`/`tb1`/`tru1` +
inflorescence-architecture branching/meristem/KRN genes; v5 coordinates); `pathway` records the
tier and protein/role each acts in.

```{r candidate-markers}
sc <- copy(mlm)[is.finite(P) & P > 0][, logP := -log10(P)]
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/en_candidate_genes.tsv"))
gg[, chr := as.integer(chr)][, qtl := ifelse(qtl_chen2019 == "" | is.na(qtl_chen2019), paste0("EN(chr", chr, ")"), qtl_chen2019)]
WIN <- 5e5
ctab <- rbindlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
  ch <- gg$chr[i]
  s <- gg$start[i]
  e <- gg$end[i]
  m <- sc[CHR == ch]
  if (!nrow(m)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  d <- pmax(0L, pmax(s - m$BP, m$BP - e))
  kc <- which.min(d)
  w <- m[BP >= s - WIN & BP <= e + WIN]
  kw <- if (nrow(w)) which.max(w$logP) else NA
  data.table(
    gene = gg$symbol[i], pathway = gg$pathway[i], chr = ch, `gene start (v5)` = s,
    `closest Δstart (bp)` = m$BP[kc] - s, `closest -log10P` = round(m$logP[kc], 2),
    `window-max -log10P` = if (nrow(w)) round(w$logP[kw], 2) else NA_real_,
    `clears perm 5%` = if (nrow(w)) (w$logP[kw] >= thr_mlm) else NA
  )
}))
setorder(ctab, chr, `gene start (v5)`)
knitr::kable(ctab,
  format.args = list(big.mark = ","),
  caption = sprintf("EN candidates (B73 v5; curated maize ear-number / prolificacy panel): closest vs window-max (±500 kb) marker, RTIGER-mosaic MLM (Family+K) scan; 'clears perm 5%%' = window-max exceeds the 1000-permutation FWER line (-log10P = %.2f).", thr_mlm)
)
```

## OLS → MLM: structure control

Here **Family** is the **82 donor-accession** fixed factor (parent-plant-level structure correction) that the MLM and per-marker OLS scans were actually computed with — a stronger correction than the 5 donor **taxa** the JLM nests on. It likely over-corrects population structure and costs some power; a taxon-level (5) MLM/OLS rerun is a pending comparison (see `TERMINOLOGY.md`).

The fixed-effect OLS (Family + marker, no K) vs the MLM (Family + K) on the RTIGER mosaic, same
fixed part. OLS is intrinsically inflated (ancestry-block LD, unfixable by any fixed factor);
K deflates λ toward 1 and removes the ancestry-correlated bulk that a near-isogenic,
teosinte-ancestry-structured ear-number panel carries.

```{r ols-manhattan, fig.width=9, fig.height=4.4}
plot_manhattan(ols,
  sprintf("ZEAL EN — OLS (Family + marker, no K), RTIGER mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = perm FWER 5%%)", lam(ols$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_ols,
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/en_gwas_ols_manhattan_fdr_snp50k.png")
)
```

The OLS Manhattan (no K) is genome-wide inflated (ancestry-block LD) — the same structure
artifact the MLM K term removes below.

```{r ols-vs-mlm}
lam_tab <- data.table(
  model = c("OLS (Family + marker, no K)", "MLM (Family + K)"),
  markers = c(nrow(ols), nrow(mlm)),
  lambda_GC = c(lam(ols$P), lam(mlm$P)),
  `n > perm 5%` = c(nsig(ols$P, thr_ols), nsig(mlm$P, thr_mlm)),
  max_logP = round(c(max(-log10(ols$P)), max(-log10(mlm$P))), 2)
)
knitr::kable(lam_tab, caption = "OLS vs MLM on the RTIGER mosaic (same Family fixed part): K deflates genomic inflation; n markers above each scan's 1000-permutation 5% FWER line.")
```

```{r ols-vs-mlm-peaks}
ov <- fread(overlap)
peak_at <- function(scan, ch, s) {
  w <- scan[CHR == ch & abs(BP - s) <= 5e5 & is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  if (!nrow(w)) NA_real_ else round(max(-log10(w$P)), 2)
}
cmp <- ov[, .(
  gene = symbol, chr,
  `OLS peak` = mapply(peak_at, list(ols), chr, start),
  `MLM peak` = mapply(peak_at, list(mlm), chr, start)
)]
cmp[, `drop (OLS−MLM)` := round(`OLS peak` - `MLM peak`, 2)]
setorder(cmp, -`OLS peak`)
knitr::kable(cmp, caption = "Local peak -log10P (±500 kb) per candidate: OLS vs MLM. K suppresses the ancestry-collinear ear-number signal.")
```

## JLM QTL genomic distribution (Panel A)

EN QTL from JLM (TASSEL StepwiseOLS, nested in taxon, entry by a 1000-permutation empirical α = 0.05 (Chen-style genome-wide FWER; per-trait)). JLM QTL are
selected by the stepwise enter/exit P (a model-selection gate), independent of the per-scan
permutation FWER line used for the GWAS Manhattans below. Model-independent — the primary EN analysis, as
in Chen 2019.

```{r lollipop-jlm, fig.width=9, fig.height=4}
p_lolli <- plot_lollipop(
  here("results/sim/zeal/tassel/en_jlm_native1.txt"), mlm,
  "ZEAL EN — JLM QTL genomic distribution (nested in taxon)",
  overlap_csv = overlap,
  out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/en_jlm_lollipop_snp50k.png")
)
p_lolli
```

## MLM (Family + K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (Panel C)

```{r manhattan-mlm, fig.width=9, fig.height=4.4}
p_mlm <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("ZEAL EN — MLM (Family + K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f; dotted = perm FWER 5%%)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_mlm, out_png = here("results/sim/zeal/en_gwas_mlm_rtiger_mosaic_manhattan_fdr_snp50k.png")
)
p_mlm
cat(sprintf("%d markers clear the 5%% FWER line (-log10P = %.2f)\n", nsig(mlm$P, thr_mlm), thr_mlm))
```

## Composite (A: JLM · B: per-SNP MLM · C: RTIGER-mosaic MLM)

Panel B (per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior)) is λ_GC-deflated — the 70%-missing per-SNP panel at 0.4×
is sparse — but catches sharp SNP hits the mosaic block-terraces away. Panel C (the ancestry
mosaic) is the usable, well-calibrated genotype. The B↔C contrast is the notebook's
per-SNP-scatter vs ancestry-block-smoothing, and the demonstration that ancestry inference is
mandatory at low coverage — identical in mechanism to the DTA/PH/EH notebooks.

```{r composite, fig.width=9, fig.height=13}
p_B <- plot_manhattan(gt,
  sprintf("EN — MLM (Family+K), per-SNP genotype (bcftools HWE-posterior) (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(gt$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_gt
)
p_C <- plot_manhattan(mlm,
  sprintf("EN — MLM (Family+K), RTIGER ancestry mosaic (λ_GC = %.2f)", lam(mlm$P)),
  overlap_csv = overlap, lod = thr_mlm
)
composite <- cowplot::plot_grid(p_lolli, p_B, p_C, ncol = 1, labels = c("A", "B", "C"), label_size = 20, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/en_composite_snp50k.png"), composite, width = 9, height = 13, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
composite
```

## Multi-caller EN scan (control · nNIL · RTIGER · LB-Impute · binhmm)

The same EN MLM (Family+K) scan on each ancestry caller's recovered genotypes, at the real
~0.4× depth (not a coverage sweep). The robust, trait-independent finding is **teosinte-presence
recovery**: the block/bin-aggregated callers (RTIGER 0.111; binhmm 0.101) recover the established
BzeaSeq fraction (~0.10), while per-SNP calling (control) and the per-SNP HMM recalls (nNIL,
LB-Impute) **under-call**. Genomic inflation is phenotype-dependent (reported per caller below).
Caller genotypes are population-level and trait-independent; only the phenotype (EN) changes.

```{r caller-table}
callers <- c(control = "hwe_post_gt", nNIL = "nnil_mosaic", RTIGER = "rtiger_mosaic", `LB-Impute` = "lbimpute_mosaic", binhmm = "binhmm_mosaic")
gg <- fread(here("data/teonam/en_candidate_genes.tsv"))[, chr := as.integer(chr)]
# best (max -log10P) EN candidate window per caller
max_cand_logp <- function(sc) {
  lp <- -log10(sc$P)
  vals <- unlist(lapply(seq_len(nrow(gg)), function(i) {
    idx <- which(sc$CHR == gg$chr[i] & abs(sc$BP - gg$start[i]) <= 5e5)
    if (length(idx)) max(lp[idx], na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_
  }))
  if (all(is.na(vals))) NA_real_ else round(max(vals, na.rm = TRUE), 2)
}
ctab2 <- rbindlist(lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  g <- callers[[nm]]
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/en_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", g)))
  mf <- here(sprintf("data/zeal/zeal_%s.rds", g))
  pres <- if (file.exists(mf)) {
    st <- readRDS(mf)$state
    round(mean(st %in% c(1L, 2L), na.rm = TRUE), 3)
  } else {
    NA_real_
  }
  sc <- sc[is.finite(P) & P > 0]
  data.table(
    caller = nm, `teosinte presence` = pres, lambda_GC = lam(sc$P),
    `n > perm 5%` = nsig(sc$P, gwas_thr(TR, "mlm", g)),
    `best candidate -log10P` = max_cand_logp(sc)
  )
}))
knitr::kable(ctab2, caption = "EN MLM (Family+K) by ancestry caller at 0.4×, with n markers above the caller's own 1000-permutation 5% FWER line and the best (max) -log10P across the EN ear-number / prolificacy candidate windows.")
```

```{r caller-composite, fig.width=9, fig.height=20}
panels <- lapply(names(callers), function(nm) {
  sc <- fread(here(sprintf("data/zeal/en_gwas_mlm_%s_donor_accession_snp50k.csv", callers[[nm]])))
  plot_manhattan(sc, sprintf("EN MLM (Family+K) — %s (λ_GC = %.2f; perm FWER 5%%)", nm, lam(sc$P)), overlap_csv = overlap, lod = gwas_thr(TR, "mlm", callers[[nm]]))
})
cc <- cowplot::plot_grid(plotlist = panels, ncol = 1, labels = LETTERS[1:5], label_size = 18, align = "v", axis = "lr")
ggsave(here("results/sim/zeal/en_caller_composite_fdr_snp50k.png"), cc, width = 9, height = 20, dpi = 200, bg = "white")
cc
```
